You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Artist. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # helper to strip surrounding quotes
    def clean(val: str) -> str:
        return val.strip('\"')

    # group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject many times
    grouped: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    sentences: list[str] = []

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Helper: order clauses so that instrument, band, then genre appear first
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    clause_order = {
        "instrument": 0,
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": 1,
        "genre": 2,
        "background": 3,
        "occupation": 4,
        "origin": 5,
        "birthDate": 6,
        "birthYear": 6,
        "birthPlace": 6,
        "deathDate": 7,
        "deathPlace": 7,
        "nationality": 8,
        "recordLabel": 9,
        "activeYearsStartYear": 10,
        "activeYearsEndYear": 11,
        "country": 12,
        "capital": 13,
        "isPartOf": 14,
        "language": 15,
        "officialLanguage": 15,
        "derivative": 16,
        "stylisticOrigin": 17,
    }

    for subj, group in grouped.items():
        # collect clause fragments for this subject together with their predicate
        raw_clauses: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
        for t in group:
            p, o = t.predicate, clean(t.object)

            if p == "birthDate":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"was born on {o}"))
            elif p == "birthYear":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"was born in {o}"))
            elif p == "birthPlace":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"was born in {o}"))
            elif p == "deathDate":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"died on {o}"))
            elif p == "deathPlace":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"died in {o}"))
            elif p == "origin":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"originates from {o}"))
            elif p == "genre":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"performs {o} music"))
            elif p == "instrument":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"plays the {o}"))
            elif p == "occupation":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is a {o}"))
            elif p == "background":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is a {o}"))
            elif p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is associated with {o}"))
            elif p == "recordLabel":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is signed to {o}"))
            elif p == "activeYearsStartYear":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"started their career in {o}"))
            elif p == "activeYearsEndYear":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"ended their career in {o}"))
            elif p == "nationality":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is {o}"))
            elif p == "capital":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"has capital {o}"))
            elif p == "country":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is in {o}"))
            elif p == "isPartOf":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"is part of {o}"))
            elif p in {"language", "officialLanguage"}:
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"speaks {o}"))
            elif p == "derivative":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"has derivative {o}"))
            elif p == "stylisticOrigin":
                raw_clauses.append((clause_order.get(p, 99), f"originated from {o}"))
            else:
                raw_clauses.append((99, f"{p} {o}"))

        # sort clauses by the predefined order
        raw_clauses.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
        clauses = [c for _, c in raw_clauses]

        # Build the sentence for this subject
        if not clauses:
            continue
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subj} {clauses[0]}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Additional sentences for genre‑relationship predicates
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    genre_predicates = {
        "musicFusionGenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a musical fusion of {s}.",
        "musicSubgenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a sub‑genre of {s}.",
        "derivative": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a derivative of {s}.",
        "stylisticOrigin": lambda s, o: f"{o} originated from {s}.",
    }

    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate in genre_predicates:
            # avoid duplicating sentences already created for person subjects
            if t.subject not in grouped:
                sentences.append(genre_predicates[t.predicate](t.subject, clean(t.object)))

    # Combine sentences for different subjects into one paragraph
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
